attack_chain.description: Attackers scanned internet-exposed applications from a Proton VPN IP and found a vulnerable application deployed in the Kubernetes cluster.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an external IP address in this case it's just a proton VPN IP which was able to connect to this this application to through just presumably scanning

evidence_chunks.quote: an external IP address in this case it's just a proton VPN IP which was able to connect to this this application to through just presumably scanning
an external IP address in this case it's just a proton VPN IP which was able to connect to this this application to through just presumably scanning

attack_chain.description: Attackers exploited a known vulnerability in an application deployed to the Kubernetes cluster that had been unintentionally exposed to the internet via a misconfigured Security Group, achieving code execution on the EC2 hosting the pod.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: once they had scanned the vulnerable application they were able to run code on the ec2 which was hosting the kubernetes Pod

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker leveraged the IAM role attached to the EC2/Kubernetes pod (inherited by the container) to access AWS APIs via the AWS CLI.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: that machine had an IM Ro associated with it which allowed access to that secret

evidence_chunks.quote: that machine had an IM Ro associated with it which allowed access to that secret
that machine had an IM Ro associated with it which allowed access to that secret

attack_chain.technique_name: Credentials from Password Stores: Cloud Secrets Management Stores
attack_chain.description: Using the overprivileged EC2 instance role, attacker retrieved the IdP's AWS access key from AWS Secrets Manager.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: that same key was accessed by some ec2 instance role from some ec2 IP address

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen IdP IAM user access key to call STS and generate session tokens mimicking legitimate IdP session naming conventions, bypassing the proprietary identity provider (and its MFA/IP restrictions).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they got the key that's used the access key used by the identity provider to create these sessions... they could generate SDS tokens they can make it look just like a session that came through the identity provider

attack_chain.description: Attacker assumed roles via STS with the faked IdP-style session names to obtain broad privileges across hundreds of AWS accounts, bypassing the IdP entirely.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they all looked like they're coming from SDS following this convention

evidence_chunks.quote: they all looked like they're coming from SDS following this convention
they all looked like they're coming from SDS following this convention

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed S3 buckets containing MongoDB backups (BSON files) with customer PII.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: almost everything the attackers had taken had come from S3 buckets

evidence_chunks.quote: almost everything the attackers had taken had come from S3 buckets
almost everything the attackers had taken had come from S3 buckets

attack_chain.description: Attackers exfiltrated hundreds of gigabytes of JSON data (MongoDB backups / customer databases) out of the AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: it was a few hundreds of gigabytes of Json just sent over from the attacker

evidence_chunks.quote: it was a few hundreds of gigabytes of Json just sent over from the attacker
it was a few hundreds of gigabytes of Json just sent over from the attacker

attack_chain.description: Attackers sent a ransom note demanding payment within 48 hours under threat of releasing the stolen data; the company negotiated and paid the ransom.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we have all your data... we will release it in 48 hours... the company paid the ransom

techniques.evidence_quote: an external IP address... which was able to connect to this this application to through just presumably scanning
this application which was deployed actually had a very well-known exploitable vulnerability in it

techniques.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
techniques.evidence_quote: this is a bash history from the machine itself

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: that machine had an IM Ro associated with it which allowed access to that secret

techniques.technique_name: Credentials from Password Stores: Cloud Secrets Management Stores
techniques.evidence_quote: that same key was accessed by some ec2 instance role
this user is the one which basically has all the Privileges because it's the only one that grants access to any other users

techniques.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
techniques.evidence_quote: they could generate SDS tokens they can make it look just like a session that came through the identity provider
they mimic the same way the identity provider worked they created an SDS session with the same convention for a session name
almost everything the attackers had taken had come from S3 buckets
a few hundreds of gigabytes of Json just sent over from the attacker
they take a lot of data and they only use that part of the extortion to demand a ransom

impact_type: Data Exfiltration and Ransom (extortion without encryption)
initial_access_vector: Exploitation of a known vulnerability in a Kubernetes-deployed application unintentionally exposed to the internet via a misconfigured Security Group.
persistence_mechanism: None explicitly described; attackers retained access via the stolen IdP IAM user access key, which could be used to mint STS tokens at will.
privilege_escalation: Leveraged overprivileged EC2 instance role to read a Secrets Manager secret containing the identity provider's IAM user access key, which held privileges to assume roles across the environment via STS.
